Title: Latent Phase-Shift Rollback: Inference-Time Error ...

Title: Fast and Forgettable: A Controlled Study of Novice...

Title: Multi-Scale Reversible Chaos Game Representation: ...

Title: New bounds for the area of MOTS and generalized ul...

Results: Thinking-QwenVL improves accuracy from 67.63% to 71.60% on StreamingBench; achieves 71.6% on StreamingBench and 46.9% on OVOBench.

Title: Random-State Generation and Preparation Complexity...
Method: Numerical simulation of random global pulse sequences applied to the Rydberg-Ising Hamiltonian on periodic chains; comparison with Haar-random states within symmetry sectors; quantum optimal control for state preparation using target states sampled from a broad entanglement entropy distribution.
Dataset: Rydberg atom arrays modeled as periodic chains under the Rydberg-Ising Hamiltonian, with varying interatomic distances (strong to weak interaction regimes) and fixed evolution times.
Metrics: level-spacing statistics of reduced density matrices, Porter-Thomas distribution deviation, entanglement entropy, entanglement spectrum statistics, distribution of measurement probabilities, fidelity (and infidelity), entanglement entropy of target states
Results: Infidelities between 10^{-5} and 3×10^{-2} achieved for 9-spin target states; fidelity decreases with increasing entanglement entropy of the target state; strong-interaction regime shows blockade-limited entanglement and non-Porter-Thomas measurement probability distribution; weaker interactions yield Haar-like statistics at long times; intermediate interactions achieve this behavior on experimentally relevant timescales.
Limitations: Fidelity decreases with entanglement entropy, indicating intrinsic difficulty in preparing highly entangled states under realistic constraints; results are numerical and limited to 9 spins and specific Hamiltonian parameters.

Title: Tell me Mr. AI, what do you see in this image?...

Title: Large-c BCFT Entanglement Entropy with Deformed Bo...
Method: Analyzing infinitesimal global conformal boundary deformations equivalent to leading-order BCFT perturbations with the boundary displacement operator; mapping to an emergent JT gravity description via transparent boundary conditions and dilaton boundary conditions set by the BCFT deformation; relying on assumptions about BCFT spectrum and OPE coefficients.

Title: Six Llamas: Comparative Religious Ethics Through L...
Method: Fine-tuning six variants of Meta-Llama-3.1-8B (one unmodified control + five LoRA-adapted on Christianity, Islam, Judaism, Hinduism, or Buddhism texts); probing with 17 standardized ethical prompts; multi-temperature sampling across ten temperature settings; computing response consistency, pairwise inter-model agreement, temperature sensitivity coefficients, and run-to-run stability.
